channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Indonesia launches free meals program to feed children and pregnant women to fight stunting
item.description: Indonesia’s new government has started an ambitious $28 million project to feed nearly 90 million children and pregnant women to fight malnutrition and stunting although critics question whether the nationwide program is affordable.
item.dc:creator: Niniek Karmini And Dita Alangkara, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands protest in Montenegro to demand ouster of top security officials over mass shooting
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Several thousand people rallied in Montenegro on Sunday demanding the resignations of top security officials over the shooting earlier this week that left 12 people dead, including two children.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shigemi Fukahori, who survived Nagasaki bombing and spent his life advocating for peace, dies at 93
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Shigemi Fukahori, a survivor of the 1945 Nagasaki atomic bombing, who devoted his life to advocating for peace and campaigning against nuclear weapons, has died. He was 93. Fukahori died at a hospital in Nagasaki, southwestern Japan, on Jan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader urges supporters to demonstrate to force Maduro to leave office
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado on Sunday urged supporters to demonstrate across the country, saying in a video message that President Nicolás Maduro will not step down on his own and they “must make him leave.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: Heavy snow and freezing rain have brought widespread disruption across Europe particularly in the U.K. and Germany with several major airports suspending flights.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ukraine presses attacks in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he will urge allies to boost his country's air defenses at a meeting this week in Germany.
item.dc:creator: Volodymr Yurchuk And Elise Morton, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Austrian Chancellor Nehammer says he will resign after talks on forming a new government fail
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian Chancellor Karl Nehammer said Saturday he will resign in the coming days after talks on forming a new government failed a second time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Honduras threatens to expel US military over Trump deportation threat
item.description: Honduran President Xiomara Castro issued President-elect Trump a stark warning earlier this week over his vow to pursue mass deportations when he returns to the White House, threatening to bar U.S. troops from the Latin American nation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces Middle East appointment: Hopefully she's learned her lesson
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Morgan Ortagus, a former State Department spokesperson, to serve as deputy special presidential envoy for Middle East peace in his next administration. Trump noted in his announcement that Ortagus previously was critical of him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Investigators meet in Brazil to extract data from black box of crashed Azerbaijani airliner
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Several of Brazilian air force investigators are working with colleagues from three other nations to analyze data from the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed in Kazakhstan on Dec. 25, killing 38 people. Azerbaijan claims the jet was unintentionally shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blasts UK energy policy and ‘windmills’ after company blames windfall tax for less drilling
item.description: President-elect Trump is criticizing the United Kingdom’s energy policy and saying it should get rid of windmills after a company blamed the country’s windfall tax for its early exit from drilling in Europe’s North Sea. In a post on his social media site Truth Social, Trump wrote, “The U.K.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza as war grinds into the new year
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: The New Year’s Eve ball dropped in soggy Times Square, where thousands of revelers stuck it out in heavy rain to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The year in review: Influential people who died in 2024
item.description: It was a life that took him from peanut farming to the presidency. While former President Jimmy Carter ’s time in the White House only lasted one term, the decades afterward were defined by humanitarian work that affected people all around the world.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD McGHEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Airline says pre-flight inspection of South Korea plane showed 'no issues': report
item.description: Investigators are in South Korea searching through plane crash debris to try to determine what caused the country's deadliest air disaster in decades, which killed 179.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US levels sanctions on entities in Iran, Russia over attempted election interference
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions against Russia and Iran on Tuesday in response to efforts by both countries to influence the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation
